capabilities: Ensure LLM outputs are free from injection-driven anomalies by evaluating and blocking unexpected instructions or policy violations.

procedure.action: Extract the LLM output by capturing the generated text immediately after model inference using a secure logging mechanism that timestamps each output using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Filter anomalies by applying a regular expression filter to identify patterns indicative of injection attacks, such as SQL commands or shell script syntax by applying a predicate filter with threshold score > 0.6 on each candidate record; discard records below threshold and log detection_count; assert false_positive_rate < 0.05.

Score detected anomalies by cross-referencing against a predefined list of safe commands and policies using a whitelist approach stored in a secure database by scoring against rubric criteria (0–3 per dimension); aggregate weighted sum; assert weighted_score >= 2.0 threshold; reject if any mandatory criterion scores < 1.

Block and log any output containing anomalies by invoking a security function that replaces the output with a warning message and logs the incident to a centralized security log using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Execute a secondary validation by running the output through a machine learning-based anomaly detection model trained on known safe and unsafe outputs, flagging any deviations by executing the operation with pre-condition validation; assert all inputs satisfy schema constraints before execution; assert operation_exit_code < 1.

summary: Procedure to evaluate and validate LLM outputs for injection-driven anomalies, ensuring outputs are safe and compliant.
title: LLM Output Injection Anomaly Validation

failureConditions: Injection attacks succeed when anomaly detection fails to recognize malicious patterns, causing unauthorized actions or data breaches.
Policy violations occur when the whitelist is outdated, allowing unsafe commands to pass through undetected, leading to compliance issues.
System logs become inconsistent when blocking mechanisms fail to log anomalies, causing gaps in security audits.
metrics: all inputs satisfy schema constraints before execution
